theory.canonical_statement: A cross-shaped stopper dividing an opening into four quadrants creates a stagnation-point geometry with symmetric pressure gradients. In cylindrical coordinates the incompressible continuity equation is
theory.technical_interpretation: A cross-shaped stopper dividing an opening into four quadrants creates a stagnation-point geometry with symmetric pressure gradients. In cylindrical coordinates the incompressible continuity equation is \[ \frac{1}{r}\frac{\partial}{\partial r}\bigl(r v_r\bigr) + \frac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0. \] Hence an increase in the downward velocity component $v_z$ forces a corresponding increase in the inward radial component $v_r$. The cross geometry therefore enforces four symmetric inflow channels that amplify radial focusing toward the central opening.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[ \frac{1}{r}\frac{\partial}{\partial r}\bigl(r v_r\bigr) + \frac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0. \] Hence an increase in the downward velocity component $v_z$ forces a corresponding increase in the inward radial component $v_r$. The cross geometry therefore enforces four symmetric inflow channels that amplify radial focusing toward the central opening.

\[
\frac{1}{r}\frac{\partial}{\partial r}(r v_r) + \frac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0
\]
\[
v_z \uparrow \;\Longrightarrow\; v_r \uparrow
\]
\[
N_{\mathrm{channels}} = 4
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This supplies the conservation statement that links vertical draw to radial intake.
This states the directional consequence of stronger downward transport in the cross geometry.
This closes the progression by identifying the structural reason the focusing is amplified rather than generic.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Primary Equation.}
\[ \frac{1}{r}\frac{\partial}{\partial r}\bigl(r v_r\bigr) + \frac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0. \] Hence an increase in the downward velocity component $v_z$ forces a corresponding increase in the inward radial component $v_r$. The cross geometry therefore enforces four symmetric inflow channels that amplify radial focusing toward the central opening.

\[
\frac{1}{r}\frac{\partial}{\partial r}(r v_r) + \frac{\partial v_z}{\partial z} = 0
\]
\[
v_z \uparrow \;\Longrightarrow\; v_r \uparrow
\]
\[
N_{\mathrm{channels}} = 4
\]

\paragraph{Primary Derivation.}
The first line specifies a map, while the subsequent line provides its explicit form \( v_z \uparrow \;\Longrightarrow\; v_r \uparrow \). Verifying that the formula satisfies the declared mapping properties confirms that the map is well defined.
\paragraph{Equation Type.}
This statement is classified as a map relation.

\paragraph{Interpretation.}
It pertains to the fields or functions just described. In the TRAWIN reading, the equation functions as the mapping carrier for the entry’s information content. According to CHL, the derivation provides a witness morphism connecting the canonical proposition to its proof certificate.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
